behavior.business_purpose: The product surface of agentic-kanban: the single screen a local operator uses to watch and steer a fleet of AI coding agents. It renders the kanban board (columns = workflow statuses, cards = tickets), lets the user create/move/edit/delete tickets by drag or keyboard, opens issue-detail and workspace/diff panels, exposes a command palette and keyboard shortcuts, and reflects live agent activity (which ticket an agent works, review/merge state, approvals) in real time without manual refresh. It is a mostly-thin optimistic projection of server truth with a few client-local UX policies (WIP visual tint, drop-position arithmetic, archive-confirm gate, column partitioning).

actors.name: operator
actors.notes: the single local user; drives every UI control

actors.name: server-push
actors.notes: agent/monitor/MCP/CLI mutations broadcast over the board WebSocket and are reflected without user action

entry_points.observable: renders status columns + cards; drag/keyboard interactions mutate the board
switches the active board view component
fuzzy action list; executes create/navigate/project-switch/workspace actions

entry_points.ref: Keyboard shortcuts (?, a, q, arrows/vim, Escape)
entry_points.observable: open overlays/panels, navigate cards, close dialogs

entry_points.ref: Issue detail panel / inline+expandable create panel
entry_points.observable: view/edit/delete a ticket; create a ticket with optional workspace launch

entry_points.ref: Workspace panel / diff viewer / settings panel
entry_points.observable: review/merge a workspace, read diff, add diff comments, change settings prefs
board_changed/session_*/approval_requested pushes drive coalesced refetch and card badges
typed REST client; throws Error carrying server error message + status, surfaced as toast/banner

behaviors.statement: The board renders one lane per configured workflow status, each holding its ticket cards, with the expected status names
behaviors.observable_outcome: named status columns appear (Todo/In Progress/Done/…); cards render under their status; header title shown

behaviors.statement: A skeleton placeholder is shown while the board API is in-flight, then replaced by real content
behaviors.observable_outcome: skeleton board visible during load; replaced by columns once data arrives

behaviors.statement: Dragging (or keyboard-advancing) a card to another column applies the move instantly and converges on the server-persisted position
preconditions: a ticket card exists in a source column
behaviors.observable_outcome: card lands in the target column immediately; server board reflects the new status/sortOrder after a debounced refetch

behaviors.statement: Moving a ticket into Done/Cancelled while it has a live (non-closed) workspace requires explicit confirmation before committing
preconditions: the ticket's main workspace is active/not-closed
behaviors.observable_outcome: a confirm dialog blocks the move; only on confirm does the PATCH+refetch run; cancel leaves the card in place

behaviors.statement: A server-rejected move rolls the card back to its exact prior position and surfaces the server's rejection message
preconditions: a move whose PATCH the server rejects (e.g. terminal-move guard)
behaviors.observable_outcome: card snaps back to source column; a toast shows the server error message; board state matches pre-move snapshot

behaviors.statement: Advancing/closing a ticket that has dependents shows a dependency-impact preview before committing
behaviors.observable_outcome: a dependency-impact preview is shown; the move proceeds only after acknowledgement

behaviors.statement: The inline create form on the Todo column creates a new ticket and it appears on the board
behaviors.observable_outcome: submitting the inline form adds the new card; cancel/Escape closes the form without creating

behaviors.statement: The inline create form can expand to a full-screen creation panel, carrying over entered state and exposing all fields incl. an optional Start-Workspace toggle
behaviors.observable_outcome: panel shows all fields; in-progress input carries over; Start Workspace reveals sub-options; submit creates; backdrop/Escape/Cancel close it; submit disabled when title empty

behaviors.statement: Clicking a card opens the issue detail panel; the operator can edit and delete the ticket, and Escape/expand-collapse control the panel layout
behaviors.observable_outcome: detail panel opens with ticket data; edits persist; delete removes the card; Escape closes; expand cycles sidebar→modal→fullscreen and collapse reverses

behaviors.statement: Board state changed server-side (agent/monitor/MCP/CLI/API) is reflected in the live UI without a manual refresh, via the WebSocket push + coalesced refetch
preconditions: a server-side issue create or status change
behaviors.observable_outcome: a ticket created via API appears on the board within seconds; a status change relocates the card to the new column

behaviors.statement: A card surfaces its workspace's live agent state — Agent working / AI reviewing / AI fixing — and shows none when idle or workspace-less
preconditions: the ticket has a workspace in a given status
behaviors.observable_outcome: the matching activity badge renders for active/reviewing/resolving states; no badge for idle or no-workspace

behaviors.statement: A column over its configured WIP limit tints its header red (advisory only — the move is never blocked); an unset/garbage limit is treated as no limit
preconditions: a WIP-limit pref set for a status
behaviors.observable_outcome: column classified under/at/over drives the header tint; zero/negative/non-numeric coerced to no-limit

behaviors.statement: The 'AI Reviewed' staging column renders only when it holds issues, or when auto-review is on without auto-merge; it appears/disappears as tickets enter/leave
behaviors.observable_outcome: column hidden when empty; appears when an issue moves in (with an awaiting-manual-merge hint); hides again when the issue leaves; always shown under autoReview&&!autoMerge

behaviors.statement: The operator switches between Board, Table, and Graph views; each renders its expected content and supports its own interactions (table sort/filter/row-open; graph zoom/legend/status-filter)
behaviors.observable_outcome: Table view shows column headers + issue rows, sortable headers reorder, status filter narrows rows, row click opens detail; Graph view renders nodes/legend, zoom scales, status filter shows/hides backlog+done; switching back restores kanban

behaviors.statement: In Graph view, Critical Path mode highlights root blockers with downstream-count badges and opens a dependency-chain side panel
behaviors.observable_outcome: Critical Path toggle visible; clicking switches to critical-path rendering; root blockers show downstream count; clicking one opens the chain panel; toggling back restores normal graph

behaviors.statement: Ctrl-clicking multiple cards multi-selects them and a bulk action (e.g. priority change) applies to the whole selection
behaviors.observable_outcome: selected cards highlight; a bulk priority change updates all selected tickets

behaviors.statement: The command palette (Ctrl+K) fuzzy-filters actions and executes them by keyboard or click, incl. project switching and context create
behaviors.observable_outcome: Ctrl+K opens palette; typing filters; arrow+Enter or click runs the action; 'New Issue + Start Workspace' opens the create panel with workspace checked; project switch changes active project and refreshes board; Escape closes

behaviors.statement: Workspace actions (Review/Merge) surface in the palette only for eligible workspaces and are gated by workspace state
preconditions: an issue with a workspace in a given state
behaviors.observable_outcome: Review/Merge appear for idle workspaces; triggering Review transitions the workspace to reviewing (toast); actions absent for active/closed/no-workspace issues

behaviors.statement: Single-key shortcuts open panels/overlays (?, a, q) and a context-aware shortcut-help overlay, while being suppressed during text entry
preconditions: board focused, not typing in an input
behaviors.observable_outcome: ? toggles a help overlay listing shortcuts (context-aware for Butler); 'a' opens All-Workspaces panel; 'q' opens Quick Tasks; single keys do NOT fire while typing in search; Escape/backdrop/repeat-? dismiss

behaviors.statement: Arrow/vim keys move a card cursor across columns and rows, seeding onto the first card and clamping at edges, skipping empty columns
behaviors.observable_outcome: the focused/cursor card moves with arrows/hjkl; clamps at column edges; skips empty columns horizontally; can advance a card to the next status

behaviors.statement: The diff viewer shows a workspace's changes (stats header, file tree, added lines, unified/split toggle) and supports full diff-comment CRUD
preconditions: a workspace with a real diff
behaviors.observable_outcome: diff stats + file tree + added lines render; unified/split toggles; comments can be created/edited/deleted via UI; comment count badge updates

behaviors.statement: The board stats bar shows per-status ticket counts and a commits counter, and a Blocked filter narrows the board to blocked issues
behaviors.observable_outcome: stats bar shows counts per status + commits; Blocked filter shows only blocked issues and toggling off restores all

behaviors.statement: The settings panel saves agent/workflow preferences that persist across reopen (agent command, profile, auto-review/auto-fix/auto-merge/board-monitoring toggles)
behaviors.observable_outcome: saved values are present on reopen; workflow toggles persist; cancel closes without saving; profile dropdown exposes harness-named options

behaviors.statement: A failed board mutation surfaces the server error to the user rather than failing silently
preconditions: a mutation whose API call fails (5xx)
behaviors.observable_outcome: an error banner/toast appears carrying the failure; the optimistic state is corrected

permissions.notes: single-user local app — no RBAC; permission dimension is near-N/A. The only permission-shaped gate is server-side approval_requested (human approves an agent tool call), surfaced as a dialog.

error_states.statement: server-rejected move snaps the card back and toasts the server message
failed mutation shows an error banner
Review/Merge gated — absent or refused when the workspace is not idle

unknowns.question: Does any test assert the archive-confirm dialog actually blocks/commits a Done move with a live workspace?
unknowns.why_unknown: no e2e among candidates exercises the confirm path; only the AI-Reviewed-column tests move issues to terminal statuses without a live workspace

unknowns.question: Is the optimistic-move rollback flicker (card snaps back + error toast) asserted anywhere?
unknowns.why_unknown: the drag e2e asserts only the success convergence via server board API; no negative/rejected-move e2e found

unknowns.question: Is the WIP-limit red header tint asserted as a rendered visual outcome?
unknowns.why_unknown: wipLimits.test.ts asserts the classifier (under/at/over) but no test reads the column header style

unknowns.question: Does the approval_requested dialog have any UI coverage?
unknowns.why_unknown: no candidate test drives an approval push to a visible dialog

unknowns.question: Is keyboard card navigation (cursor move + focus) covered end-to-end in the running UI?
unknowns.why_unknown: boardKeyboardNav.test.ts covers the pure computeNavTarget function only; no e2e drives the actual focus/cursor

packages/e2e/tests/ui/board.test.ts::shows header with title
packages/e2e/tests/ui/skeleton-board.test.ts::skeleton is shown while the board API is in-flight, then replaced by content
packages/e2e/tests/ui/board.test.ts::drag issue between columns
packages/client/src/lib/issueMoveHelpers.test.ts::moveIssueToStatus (8 cases)
packages/client/src/lib/issueMoveHelpers.test.ts::applyLocalReorder

history.note: drag e2e asserts convergence via server board API, not UI snapshot; pure transform fully unit-tested

gap.rationale: No e2e moves a ticket WITH a live workspace into Done/Cancelled to exercise the confirm dialog (block on cancel, commit on confirm). ai-reviewed-column tests move issues to terminal statuses but without a live workspace, so the gate never fires. High blast radius (risks discarding in-flight agent work).

The drag e2e covers only the happy path. No test forces a server-rejected move (e.g. terminal-move guard) and asserts the card snaps back to the exact prior column + the server error message is shown. This is the optimistic layer's core safety property.
No candidate test advances/closes a ticket with dependents to assert the dependency-impact preview is shown before commit.

permission.note: near-N/A: single-user local app, no RBAC

top_gaps.rationale: The optimistic-move layer's core safety property — a server-rejected move snaps the card back to its exact prior column and surfaces the server message — is entirely unverified. High blast radius (BoardPage is the hottest file, 718 commits; move legality is server-authoritative). Force a 4xx on PATCH /api/issues via page.route, drop a card, assert it returns to source + error toast text. Reuses the existing drag-dispatch helper in board.test.ts.

Moving a ticket with a LIVE workspace into Done/Cancelled must show a blocking confirm (risks discarding in-flight agent work). No e2e exercises the gate — the only terminal-move tests (ai-reviewed-column) lack a live workspace. Seed an issue with an active/mock workspace, attempt the archive move, assert the confirm dialog blocks on cancel and only commits on confirm.

The 'status changes via API' realtime test asserts the SERVER board, not that the rendered card relocated (touches-only on the UI). On an agent-driven board this live-reflection is the headline value. Strengthen to assert the card visibly moves columns in the UI after an out-of-band PATCH, and add a burst (multiple rapid pushes) to exercise the coalescing/seq-guard against the live DOM.

computeNavTarget is unit-pure-tested but nothing asserts arrow/vim keys actually move the focused card in the running UI — the keyboard-operability a11y outcome. A thin e2e: focus board, press ArrowRight/j, assert the highlighted/selected card changed.

WIP classifier (under/at/over + boundary coercion) is unit-tested; the visible red-tint outcome on the column header is unasserted. Set a wip_limit pref below a column's count, assert the header carries the 'over' visual state. Low blast radius (advisory-only policy).

summary.cut_line: Emitted P1–P3. Below the line (not emitted): board-ui.move.dependencyPreview (P2, dependency-impact preview untested) and palette workspace-action failure-path error handling (P4) — real but lower ROI than the rollback/archive/realtime trio.
